stem: A electronics testing study measures the battery life (hours) for a sample of 30 smartphone models. The sample mean is 211.9 hours with a sample standard deviation of 77.0 hours. However, 2 of the 30 observations appear to be outliers (values more than 2.5 standard deviations from the mean). If these outliers are removed, the remaining 28 observations have a mean of 198.5 hours and standard deviation of 55.6 hours. Estimate the true population mean and provide a 95% confidence interval that accounts for the uncertainty about whether outliers should be included.

ground_truth.answer: With all data: mean = 211.9, 95% CI = [183.2, 240.7]. Without outliers: mean = 198.5, 95% CI = [177.5, 219.5]. Combined interval accounting for analytic uncertainty: [177.5, 240.7]. Point estimate: 205.2 hours.

solution_steps: The outlier treatment choice creates structural uncertainty. Combined interval: [177.5, 240.7].
common_failure_modes: Using only the full sample without considering outlier impact
Removing outliers without reporting both analyses
Not recognizing that the choice of analysis method is itself a source of uncertainty
